title: DexGello: Arm-Hand Decoupled Teleoperation
description: Yongxi Lai-led Franka + Wuji dexterous-hand data-collection project using GELLO, MANUS, and VIVE as decoupled arm, hand-pose, and finger channels.
summary: DexGello separates robot-oriented arm control from human-oriented hand pose and finger articulation: GELLO controls/logs q_arm for Franka, VIVE logs independent T_hand on the operator hand, and MANUS logs/retargets q_finger for Wuji hand. The latest single-hand GELLO + MANUS run is qualitatively promising, but arm-joint behavior can still deviate and dual-hand calibration is not currently practical, so the next key check is a direct comparison against the revised VIVE Tracker path.

subprojects.label: A
subprojects.title: Arm channel
subprojects.body: GELLO gives low-latency, kinematically matched arm control and robot joint records.

subprojects.label: B
subprojects.title: Hand-pose channel
subprojects.body: VIVE Tracker on the operator hand records independent human hand pose, not another Franka control input.

subprojects.label: C
subprojects.title: Finger channel
subprojects.body: MANUS captures full finger articulation and drives/logs Wuji dexterous-hand retargeting.

asset_alt: DexGello arm hand finger decoupled teleoperation architecture
asset_caption: DexGello: GELLO -> q_arm, VIVE -> T_hand, MANUS -> q_finger, synchronized into one dexterous demonstration record.
details: Lead / ownership: Yongxi Lai leads this project; Boris provides dashboard / research organization and real-robot integration support.

Motivation: GELLO is strong for accurate, low-latency robot-arm teleoperation because the leader arm mirrors the target arm kinematics. For dexterous manipulation, however, the human wrist/hand/finger semantics are compressed if everything is treated as only robot end-effector or joint state.

Core representation: each demo should record (q_arm, T_hand, q_finger). q_arm is the GELLO / Franka arm configuration, T_hand is the calibrated VIVE pose of the operator's hand-back tracker, and q_finger is MANUS finger articulation retargeted or logged for Wuji hand.

Front-line update 2026-06-16 from the Boris <-> Yongxi Lai chat: the immediate blocker is wrist semantics rather than generic bring-up. GELLO wrist mapping to Franka is still off by about 90 degrees, and VIVE wrist retargeting is still hard to tune. The likely next step is a redesigned printed wrist part / linkage so the glove-to-gripper relation matches palm pose rather than fingertip pose, with Wuhao helping sketch the part. Current control split under discussion: keep GELLO for the direct joint-correspondence arm path, and evaluate whether wrist motion should instead come from VIVE despite the current retargeting difficulty.

Front-line update 2026-06-17 from the Boris <-> Yongxi Lai chat: Yongxi ran the pure GELLO + MANUS single-hand setup and it looked broadly as expected in the shared 15 s video evidence, but the GELLO arm joint behavior can still be unintuitive / off expectation. Current limitation: the setup is effectively single-hand only; for dual-hand operation, the calibration procedure is not workable right now. Boris asked for a direct comparison against the VIVE Tracker version; Yongxi said the VIVE path has been revised again and should be tested next. Safety note from the same chat: live testing may need to wait for Jingru / onsite support because Singapore staffing is thin.

Key design decision: VIVE should not be treated as an extra Franka command channel in v0. It is an independent human hand-pose measurement used to quantify what GELLO end-effector pose misses, how much overlap exists, and whether arm-hand decoupling improves usability and data quality.

System path: start from the already completed Franka + Wuji + GELLO base, then finish MANUS glove streaming, VIVE tracker pairing / serial listing, time sync, extrinsic calibration, and a replay verifier that can reconstruct one aligned DexGello episode.

Demo boundary update 2026-06-13: physical demo scenarios such as Wuji + Franka slime-clay cutting and Jenga-style block extraction moved to Engineering / Real-Robot Demos. DexGello keeps the reusable data/control stack: q_arm, T_hand, q_finger, MANUS/VIVE integration, episode logging, ablations, policy evaluation, and cuRobo planning dependency.

Research comparison: evaluate Gello-only, Gello + MANUS, and Gello + VIVE + MANUS. The expected useful signals are operator comfort, trajectory smoothness, contact timing, finger-pose quality, hand-pose alignment, demo diversity, and downstream imitation-learning / diffusion-policy performance.

Failure-data learning update 2026-06-13: DexGello should not treat all failed rollouts as negative examples to learn wholesale. The useful target is a phase-importance metric for long-horizon dexterous tasks: identify meaningful pre-failure stages worth imitating, downweight the actual failure stage, and use DAgger human-takeover segments as high-importance supervision. Example intuition: during grasp, object/contact metrics should spike; during rollout, human intervention/takeover marks that the current stage is important or risky.

P2GI proximity update 2026-06-15: KAIST's proximity-perception grasping system is a useful reference for the failure/phase metric. It suggests explicit pre-contact features such as hand-object distance, center offset, object-size estimate, grasp-posture confidence, and contact-onset expectation. DexGello should record or estimate these features when possible, but proximity sensors are not a v0 blocker.

Long-horizon demo update 2026-06-13: candidate true-robot data sources for learn-from-failure are broom-to-dustpan sweeping and two 90-degree cube turns. They are deliberately longer than simple grasp demos and should expose phase boundaries such as tool grasp, alignment, sweep/rotation, stabilization, and final placement/orientation failure.

Relation to DexUMI: DexUMI uses the human hand as a universal dexterous interface and bridges visual/kinematic gaps. DexGello keeps the efficient GELLO arm channel instead of replacing arm teleop with only hand tracking, and adds an explicit hand-pose channel so the dataset can study arm-hand information overlap.

Relation to VR teleoperation: the separate Real Robot Infra task for VR-controlling Franka remains a hardware/control baseline. DexGello's v0 should stay cleaner: Gello controls arm, MANUS controls/logs fingers, VIVE logs hand pose for data and analysis.

Completed input-stack decision 2026-06-11: GELLO distal trigger path is now treated as ACE-style wrist ring + MANUS hand-pose input inside DexGello. GELLO remains arm joint-space teleop; MANUS supplies hand/finger pose; the wrist ring is wrist/intent hardware, not a separate hand-pose camera route.

Control stack update 2026-06-12: Franka cuRobo migration is a DexGello planning/control TODO because it directly gates safe, collision-aware Franka + Wuji demo execution and replayable DexGello episodes.

Placement update 2026-06-12: the completed Wuji/Franka camera flange and DexCap-style MANUS/Wuji glove camera adapter records moved here because they directly support the DexGello Franka + Wuji + MANUS/VIVE data path.

references.title: GELLO: A General, Low-Cost, and Intuitive Teleoperation Framework for Robot Manipulators
references.notes: Primary arm-teleoperation reference. GELLO uses a kinematically equivalent low-cost leader arm for intuitive joint-space control and has demonstrated Franka / UR5 / xArm systems.

references.title: GELLO software
references.notes: Implementation reference for leader-arm robot control, calibration, and data-collection hooks.

references.title: GELLO mechanical design
references.notes: Hardware reference for low-cost leader construction and maintainable arm-teleop rigs.

references.title: DexUMI: Using Human Hand as the Universal Manipulation Interface for Dexterous Manipulation
references.notes: Closest dexterous-data motivation reference: human-hand interface, visual adaptation, retargeting, tactile/haptic considerations, and downstream policy learning.

references.title: MANUS robotics: high precision hand tracking for dexterous manipulation
references.notes: MANUS reference for high-DoF finger tracking, low-latency streaming, robot-hand teleoperation, and Isaac Lab / simulation integration.

references.title: VIVE Wrist Tracker for developers
references.notes: Tracker reference for lightweight wrist/object tracking, 6DoF pose, CAD/mounting, and OpenXR / OpenVR ecosystem considerations.

references.title: dex-teleop: VR-based teleoperation for dexterous manipulation
references.notes: Engineering reference for VIVE Tracker + hand tracking + synchronized HDF5 recording. Use as a comparison point, but DexGello keeps GELLO as the arm-control channel.

references.title: Proximity Perception-Based Grasping Intelligence
references.notes: Reference for proximity-based shared-autonomy grasping. Use as a metric/design reference for pre-contact hand-object relation and stage importance; do not make additional proximity hardware mandatory for DexGello v0.

risks_decisions: Do not collapse the project into generic VR teleoperation. The novelty is the decoupled semantic record: q_arm from GELLO, T_hand from VIVE, and q_finger from MANUS.
Calibration risk: T_hand only becomes useful if VIVE world frame, Franka base, GELLO state, camera streams, and MANUS hand root share a reproducible time-sync and extrinsic-calibration procedure.
Mounting risk: a back-of-hand tracker must be rigid enough for pose analysis while not blocking MANUS glove motion, Wuji retargeting, camera mounts, cables, or emergency operation.

Information-overlap risk: VIVE hand pose may be redundant with GELLO end-effector pose for some tabletop tasks. The project needs explicit overlap metrics rather than assuming more sensors always help.

Live-update risk 2026-06-17: pure GELLO + MANUS is now a promising single-hand baseline, but it should not be promoted to the full v0 path until joint-behavior deviations, dual-hand calibration infeasibility, and safety staffing are addressed or explicitly scoped out.

Policy risk: downstream gains may appear only after enough dexterous tasks involve wrist roll, hand approach posture, or human finger pre-shaping. Choose tasks where the extra human-hand channel has a plausible role.

Failure-data risk: failed trajectories are not automatically useful. The metric must separate high-value pre-failure phases from the actual wrong action, otherwise imitation learning may amplify bad recovery/contact behavior.

Proximity/P2GI boundary: use P2GI as a design reference for pre-contact object-relation metrics. Adding proximity sensors to Wuji is a possible v1 experiment, not a prerequisite for the current GELLO + MANUS + VIVE logger.
DexGello input boundary: do not reintroduce GELLO trigger as the hand/finger control source. Keep arm control on GELLO, wrist/intent on ACE-style ring, and hand/finger retargeting on MANUS.

Demo boundary: slime-clay cutting, Jenga extraction, broom-to-dustpan sweeping, cube-turning, plastic-bag rubbing, and Lego tool manipulation are owned by Engineering / Real-Robot Demos; DexGello contributes reusable logging, calibration, planning, and evaluation dependencies only. Safety gates still apply before any live run.

cuRobo boundary: planning wrapper, collision scene, joint limits, and reusable planner integration belong in DexGello; concrete demo scenario ownership and pass/fail evidence live in Real-Robot Demos.

Mounting dependency boundary: glove/camera adapters that affect MANUS, Wuji, VIVE, Franka, or DexGello episode replay belong in DexGello; shared robot availability and safety gates remain in Real-Robot Infra.

timeline.target_note: First resolve wrist mapping / retargeting and get one replayable DexGello episode before scaling task collection.
